name: class-wrapped-script
smell: a 50-line linear flow wrapped in a class with __init__/run()/main()
instead: top-to-bottom flow with `if __name__ == '__main__':` as the entry point

name: just-in-case-exports
smell: internal utilities exposed 'in case someone needs them'
instead: every export is a forever commitment — keep the public surface minimal

name: utils-module
smell: a module named utils or helpers
instead: name modules by what they do, not by where leftovers landed

name: fat-controller
smell: business rules living in the request handler
instead: rules belong in the service layer; the controller parses, calls, formats

name: sql-in-service
smell: SQL strings or ORM calls inside service methods
instead: the repository encapsulates the store; services call the repository interface

name: anemic-domain-model
smell: domain objects that are mostly getters and setters
instead: put behavior on the object (`order.total()`), or admit it's data-centric and reclassify

name: framework-in-domain
smell: ORM annotations or framework imports on domain entities
instead: persistence mapping lives in infrastructure; the domain stays framework-free

name: rpc-in-disguise
smell: a handler that synchronously calls three services and rolls back on failure
instead: emit the event and let handlers react; compensation is eventual, not transactional

name: mega-stage
smell: one stage that filters AND enriches AND aggregates
instead: three stages composed; each stage does exactly one transformation

name: core-imports-plugin
smell: the core package importing any plugin package
instead: dependencies point only from plugin to core, mediated by the extension-point interface

name: speculative-generality
smell: an abstraction built for a future that isn't confirmed
instead: rule of three: three cases today, or one plus a confirmed second

name: monolith-file
smell: a 3000-line file because 'it started simple'
instead: classify the archetype and split along its module lines before adding more

name: pattern-by-familiarity
smell: Repository-Service-Controller stack on a script with two database calls
instead: structure is a function of what the code is and what changes about it — classify first

name: shadow-state
smell: a component copies store data into local state and hand-syncs it
instead: read through a selector; if it must diverge it is not the same state

name: impure-reducer
smell: network calls, timers, or randomness inside a reducer/state-holder
instead: push effects to the effect layer; keep state transitions pure

name: god-viewmodel
smell: one state-holder backing multiple unrelated screens
instead: one state-holder per screen/navigation destination

name: cross-feature-import
smell: feature A imports feature B internals directly
instead: route through the shell navigation or the slice public API only

name: shared-dumping-ground
smell: shared/ fills with business-specific helpers
instead: a helper that encodes a domain concept becomes its own slice

name: mega-feature-module
smell: one feature module accretes ten unrelated screens
instead: cut at one user-facing capability per module

name: leaky-organism
smell: a library component imports an app hook or calls an app API
instead: pass data via props; the library stays ignorant of consumers

name: token-drift
smell: features hardcode hex/px values beside the token system
instead: consume tokens exclusively; add a token before a one-off value

name: distributed-monolith
smell: services split over the network but sharing one database or deploying in lockstep
instead: database-per-service and independent deploy pipelines, or admit the monolith

name: chatty-services
smell: one user request fanning into many synchronous inter-service calls
instead: async events or precomputed read views instead of call chains

name: hidden-control-flow
smell: the runner special-cases tool names to decide what happens next
instead: keep the runner generic; branching lives in the model tool choice

name: unbounded-loop
smell: no max-iteration, cost, or stop-condition check
instead: an explicit runner-checked stop condition every iteration

name: god-orchestrator
smell: the orchestrator embeds worker domain logic
instead: the orchestrator only plans, dispatches, and synthesizes

name: cross-worker-chatter
smell: workers calling each other or sharing a mutable scratchpad
instead: all inter-worker information routes through orchestrator synthesis

name: sequential-forced
smell: dynamic multi-agent decomposition applied to strictly sequential work
instead: use a pipeline or prompt chain; reserve fan-out for independent subtasks

name: copy-pasted-environments
smell: dev/staging/prod folders hand-copying the same resource blocks
instead: DRY into shared modules; environments are thin parameter sets

name: monolithic-state-blob
smell: all environments and resources in one state file
instead: isolate state per environment-layer instantiation

name: manual-drift
smell: hand-editing live resources managed by the declarative tree
instead: every change lands as a commit; the reconciler is the only writer

name: shadow-feature-logic
smell: serving reimplements feature computation in a second codebase
instead: one shared transformation definition materialized to both stores

name: point-in-time-leakage
smell: training joins features without event-time cutoffs
instead: point-in-time-correct retrieval from the feature store

name: direct-to-prod-deploy
smell: a notebook pushes its artifact straight to serving
instead: deploys come only from the versioned, approval-gated model registry

name: god-system
smell: one system reading/writing far more component types than its concern
instead: narrow systems touching the minimum component set

name: behavior-in-component
smell: components growing update logic
instead: components stay pure data; logic lives in systems
